entry.title: Cross-company Strock transfer order with Advanced return management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Most of the large companies run SAP and some data shows that 75% of world’s transaction happen in SAP. Most of these companies operate in several countries and there are transfer of their products from one country to another. This transfer of materials can occur through

  • classic intercompany or advanced intercompany stock transfer
  • advanced intercompany sales and advanced intercompany stock transfer

as shown in below image.

However, there may be situations that receiving company wants to return the received materials to the delivering company for different reasons like poor quality, over-delivery etc. SAP S/4HANA provides the option of cross-company stock transfer order to return the material to the delivering company using Advanced return management (ARM) as shown in below image.

Process starts with a

  1. Return cross-company stock transfer order with a flag on ‘Return item’

2. Outbound delivery is created from the return STO.

3. Inbound delivery is created from SPED output of the outbound delivery

4. Goods receipt happens with movement type 673 and stock moves to Quality inspection

5. Inspection certifies the material as OK

6. Stock is moved to Free available stock as Logistic follow up activity

7. Credit memo is created with respect to the inbound delivery

ARM also provides a Return overview with all the relevant documents and their statuses.

ARM provides an integrated and transparent return with automatic logistical follow up activities for all possible return scenarios.ARM also works well with EWM managed warehouses.

This blog is based on my personal insights, observation, and tests in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release in my preparation for Workshop on Reverse Logistics for Advanced intercompany flows for one of my projects. Will appreciate your views / comment and feedback.

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method for Service Contracts
entry.content:
Co Author: @Narinder_Hartala 

In this blog, we will focus on the Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Service Contract with Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method (Method 10).

First, Let's look at the Integration Overview of Service Contract Management with Event-based Revenue Recognition - billing create from Service Contract

 

Posting Logic:

Let’s assume that the following parameters are defined for your service contract:

  • Service contract for 1 year (January 1 – December 31)

  • Net value: EUR 1,200.00

  • Billing yearly

  • Billing Amount: EUR 1,200.00 (Billing Date = Contract Start Date = January 1)

The following graphic shows you the process steps for this method and the corresponding postings:

Configuration:

  • CCS: Allows you to use time-based recognition of revenue based on the billing plan for the end-to-end process of the service contract (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

  • CCSA: Allows you to recognize revenue time-based according to the billing plan based on a calendar year of 365 days. Costs are considered as they occur (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

  • CCSC: Allows you to recognize revenue time-based according to the billing plan based on a calendar year of 360 days. Costs are considered as they occur (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

In this blog, we will use revenue recognition key CCS for the service contract.

Demo:

1. Create Service Contract

Contract period for 1 year start from August, 2024 - August 2025 with Yearly Settlement.

Contract created and released.

2. Create Billing

After billing was created, run the Service Plan / Actual Report. We will see the revenue posted for the whole amount of this 1 year contract.

3. Run Revenue Recognition - Service Document

After Revenue Recognition Job completed, run the Service - Plan / Actual Report again.

Now, we can see that the revenue posting from Event-based is shown.

4. Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Service Document. Now we can see that there is Revenue Adjustment posting and Deferred Revenue Posting. Therefore, the Recognized Margin for this period 8 is 1000 USD

5. Create Service Order and do the Time confirmation

Link the Service Order to the Service Contract

Note that the service order that created under service contract will not be relevant for billing (As the billing should only be done from service contract)

Then with Advance Execution, Maintenance Order was created automatically from Service Order

We can see the link between Service Contract and Maintenance Order in Service Order History

Now we perform the time confirmation to the PM Order

After we have some expenses posted from the PM Order, we can go and check the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Document again. Now, we can see the cost from the PM Order reflexing in the service contract as well.

6. We will run revenue recognition - Service Document for period 9

7. Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Document after run the revenue recognition for period 9. Now we should have 1000 more in recognized revenue for period 9.

And then the posting will continue to period 10, 11 and so on until it is completed the yearly period. This would help in spreading the revenue cross the year instead of recognized once in one particular period and create spike of the Margin. This is the purpose of using the Event-Based Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method for Service Contracts.

For other related SAP Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog that I have wrote earlier, you can refer to following

Event-based Revenue Recognition for Service Documents:

  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Service (Advanced Execution) - Time & Expense-based invoice EBRR

Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog for Projects:

  • Posting of Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Sales (4GQ)

Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog for Sales Orders:

  • Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (1K2)
  • Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (1K2) with Recognition Key - SFSCCM

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hey Everyone!

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 The ABAP sessions at this year's SAP TechEd event will be centered around "Clean Core Extensibility powered by ABAP Cloud and Generative AI". Attendees can look forward to a range of lectures (incl. roadmaps) and jump-starts on clean core development and transformation with ABAP Cloud and GenAI for all SAP S/4HANA editions, both cloud and on-premise. Below is an overview of the SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 sessions related to clean core extensibility and ABAP Cloud. 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024



Keynotes

KEY100CEST & KEY100EDT | SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
[Executive Keynote] At SAP TechEd, experts, developers, IT leaders, and our SAP Community gather to learn, share, and build together. Hear firsthand from SAP Board Members Juergen Mueller and Muhammad Alam how innovations in SAP Build solutions, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and other solutions across our end-to-end portfolio are revolutionizing the tech world.´
Speakers: 
      • Juergen Mueller, Chief Technology Officer, SAP
      • Muhammad Alam, SAP Product Engineering, SAP
Schedule: 
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 9:00 AM - 10:30 AM CEST [KEY100CEST]
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CEST [KEY100EDT]

DEV101A & DEV101B | Keynote recap + Community welcome
[Developer Keynote] Join the Developer Advocates as they welcome the SAP Community to SAP TechEd, provide a short recap of the key announcements, and share insights into what it means for developers. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 10:55 AM CEST [DEV101A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 5:30 PM - 5:55 PM CEST [DEV101B]

DEV100A  & DEV100B | Developer keynote [deconstructed]: Keeping the promise
[Developer Keynote Deconstructed] Hear from the developer advocates for a quick injection of news, recaps, and deep-dive demos covering various announcements from the event as well as other fun stuff. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST [DEV100A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST [DEV100B]

Track Overviews 

AD100 – Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions
[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, discover how to seamlessly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth. 
Speakers: Dr Alexander Rother (SAP), Thomas Volmering (SAP), Björn Schulz (REWE digital GmbH)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

DT100 – Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
[Track Overview, 45 min] Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.
Speakers: Jan Gilg (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 11:15 AM CEST

Lectures

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build 
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions. 
Speakers: Dr. Anne Keller (SAP), Tim Back (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

AD107 – Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer capabilities
[Lecture, 25 min] Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks. 
Speakers: Dr. Jasmin Gruschke (SAP), Sebastian Baskovich (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

AD106 – Clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA with the latest ABAP Cloud features
[Lecture, 25 min] ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions on both SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP S/4HANA, helping ensure clean core compliance by definition. Learn about the latest features offered in ABAP Cloud and how generative AI can boost the development efficiency. 
Speaker: Volker Drees (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

DT201 – Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] The extensibility portfolio for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition consist both on-stack and side-by-side extension techniques. In this lecture you will learn how on-stack key user extensibility can be used to develop a custom business object together with an application in SAP Build Apps. 
Speaker: Dr. Thomas Schneider (SAP), Alda Dollani (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 2:30 PM - 2:55 PM CEST

DT200 – Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.
Speaker:  Olga Dolinskaja (SAP), Thomas Fiedler (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:00 PM - 3:25 PM CEST

Jump-Starts

AD180 – Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud powered by Joule's ABAP Developer capabilities
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model supported by generative AI. You will also learn about features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only treeviews for displaying hierarchical data. 
ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building clean core compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on-premise. 
Speaker: Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

DT280 – Building Clean Core Extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to speed up the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensions with ABAP Cloud using two new ABAP Development Tools (ADT) based generators for adding extension fields and for building custom UI services on top of business objects that have been released by SAP. 
Speaker: Andre Fischer (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024 and watch the replays of last year's events, SAP TechEd Virtual in 2023 and SAP's Devtoberfest in 2023.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: Where can I learn how to create, plan, and execute investment projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Investment Projects in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.


Overview
In this Learning Journey you will learn how to create, plan and execute investment projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project System. You will experience the end-to-end business process, from creation of projects structures, planning of costs and budgeting, to period-end settlement of projects.

Learning objectives

After completing this Learning Journey you will be able to:

  • Create investment projects using templates.
  • Create and maintain Work Breakdown Structures (WBS).
  • Carry out cost planning of investment projects.
  • Carry out budgeting of investment projects.
  • Release and execute investment projects.
  • Run period-end closing activities and settle projects to asset accounting.
  • Use reporting features in SAP S/4HANA to analyzes structure and costs of investment projects.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Basics and configuration in Service with Advanced Execution Concept in SAP S/4HANA Service
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution

Service with Advanced Execution process is used for services which require extensive planning and execution. The core characteristics of this process is the integration of the Service, which is responsible for the commercial part of the process, with the Maintenance Management, which is used for planning and execution of the service. In this process Service is also integrated with Financial Accounting which is responsible to ensure an accurate recording of cost and revenue that occurs in the service transaction.

The goal of this blog post to introduce the basics concept and the configuration of integration part which are required for the Service with Advanced Execution scenario.

Basic concepts in Service with Advanced Execution

Basic concepts

In the Service with Advanced Execution process both Service and Maintenance Management have specific roles.

Service is responsible for the commercial part of the process:

  • Service contract management and price agreement
  • Service order quotation and service order management
  • Pricing and credit check
  • Billing

Maintenance Management is responsible for planning and execution of service:

  • Maintenance task list
  • Maintenance order and confirmation management
  • Maintenance planning with strategy
  • Resource scheduling
  • Advance Shipment

The Service with Advanced Execution can be used in the following scenarios:

  • Preventive Service
    in this scenario Service is responsible to set up a regular service based on the service agreements and Maintenance Management is responsible for the planning and execution based on a maintenance order.
  • Corrective Service
    in this scenario the price (estimated- or final cost) of service is calculated based on the service requirement and sent to the customer in a service quotation for approval. If the service quotation is accepted by the customer a service order is created. The planning and execution are done in a maintenance order.
  • Billable Maintenance
    in this scenario the maintenance needs to be billed to the customer. The maintenance order is created to plan and execute the service and to allow the possibility of billing. The service order is used to generate the billing document after the service is completed.

The item which is created in Service with Advanced Execution process is called Execution Order Item. The Execution Order Item has the following features:

  • Billing relevance
    Depending on the billing scenario the following billing relevance can be set up for an execution order item:
    • Not relevant for billing
    • Summarized Billing
    • Itemized Billing
    • Billing on Completion
    • Billing via Billing Plan After Release
  • Dynamic Items
    DIP profile is entered in the Execution Order Item in the Service Order and used to determine the items and lines in the Debit Memo Request during billing. (The DIP profile is mandatory when the billing relevance is Summarized or Itemized Billing.
  • Planning Plant and Service Organization
    Planning plant and work center are available as additional fields in the execution order item of the service order quotation and service order. These fields are mandatory in the maintenance order and are copied over from the execution order item.
  • Maintenance Order Assignment Block
    The billable Maintenance Order is linked with the Execution Order Item and the Maintenance Order is displayed in the Transaction History in the item details.
  • Advanced Variant Configuration
    Configurable products can be used as Execution Order Items in Service Order Management and in General Maintenance Task List in billable Maintenance Order.
  • Credit Check
    Credit check feature can be used for Execution Order Items  in fixed price billing scenario for the billing relevance Billing on Completion.
  • Intercompany Service Orders
    Execution Order Items can be created as part of Intercompany Service Orders for business transaction between two companies with different company codes that belong to the same organization.

Basic configuration in Service with Advanced Execution process

Integration between Service and Maintenance Management

In order to activate Service with Advanced Execution integrated with Maintenance Management the mapping between Transaction Types and Item Categories used in Service and Order Types used in Maintenance Management has to be specified in Customizing under Service -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Map Order Types

By this Customizing activity the mapping between Service with Advanced Execution and Maintenance Management can be set up.
Under Service to Maintenance Management the transaction type and item category used in Service can be mapped to Maintenance Order type used in Maintenance Management. This mapping is used to determine the Maintenance Order Type during the creation of a Maintenance Order corresponding to an execution order item.
Under Maintenance Management to Service the Maintenance Order Type can be mapped to the transaction type and item category used in Service. By this customizing setting the correct transaction type and item category can be determined during the creation of Service Order corresponding to the Maintenance Order.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Map Priorities

By this Customizing activity the service priorities can be mapped to the plant maintenance priorities. This mapping is used in integration scenarios such as the creation of Maintenance Orders from Service Orders.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Define Text Types

With this Customizing activity is possible to define text type which can be used to transfer information from the Service Order to the Maintenance Order.

Integration between Service and Finance

The Customizing activity Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service needs to be enabled to allow to use Service with Advanced Execution process.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service

With this Customizing activity accounting can be activated for Service based on a dedicated cost object. In Item-Based Accounting the service transaction items are recognized as account assignment objects. The costs and revenues are posted to these objects.

By setting the switch to True an accounting based on service transaction items is enabled as dedicated account assignment objects for service transactions.

Ad Hoc Billing for Service with Advanced Execution

Unlike the rules of the periodic or milestone billing plan, Ad Hoc Billing enables to define values and dates freely. The feature can be used for the Service with Advanced Execution scenario is called Billing of Remaining Net Value. This feature enables the system to generate a billing request line with all remaining net value of the Execution Order Item automatically.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Define Billing Plan Types

By this Customizing activity the billing type Ad Hoc Billing can be defined for Service. By using Ad Hoc Billing different amounts at specified dates can be billed to the customer. The system generates on e billing request line for each billing date.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Assign Billing Plan Type to Item Category

In this Customizing activity the billing plan type Service: Ad Hoc Billing can be assigned to the item category MSI1 Maint. Service Item which is used in the Maintenance Order.

 

For more information about Service with Advanced Execution process check out the following blog post:

Service with Advanced Execution - SAP S/4HANA Clou... - SAP Community

entry.title: Electronic Invoices for Malaysia: A Guide for Communication Settings
entry.content:
You are in the process of implementing electronic invoices for Malaysia. One of the key implementation activities is to integrate your business system (for example, SAP S/4HANA Cloud) with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. The cloud edition acts as a bridge between your business system and the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (IRBM), the tax authorities in Malaysia. Among other settings, you must configure communication settings in the Manage Communication Settings - External Parties app in the cloud edition to connect to the IRBM. This blog guides you through configuring these communication settings.

Getting Started

Start your journey by reading the following documentation, which describes how to configure communication settings:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Managing Communication Settings | SAP Help Portal 
  • SAP S/4HANA: Managing Communication Settings | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP ERP: Managing Communication Settings | SAP Help Portal

The communication settings primarily involve maintaining the credentials that you've obtained from the IRBM or another authority, including a pair of client ID and client secret as well as a digital certificate. You should get these credentials ready for use before proceeding to configure communication settings in the cloud edition. You can find out the Malaysia-specific settings from the Process-Specific Settings page:

Product Guide: Process Specific Settings

Step 1: Create a New Communication Type

You need to first retrieve your taxpayer identification number (TIN) as described at How to retrieve and verify company TIN number? and then create a new communication type using this TIN.

Create communication type


Step 2: Add an Authentication Setting for Consuming the IRBM APIs

For the communication type created in step 1, add an authentication setting that is used to consume the IRBM APIs, as follows:

  1. Select the Basic type.
  2. Enter your client ID as the username and your client secret as the password.
    See How to obtain Client ID and Client Secret?.

Add basic authentication type

Step 3: Add an Authentication Setting for Signing Electronic Invoices

For the communication type created in step 1, add another authentication setting that is used to sign electronic invoices, as follows:

  1. Select the Key Pair X.509 type.
  2. Upload the certificate you generated from one of the certified service providers.
  3. Enter the password for your digital certificate.
    You need to purchase a digital certificate from a certificate authority (CA) in Malaysia. See How to acquire Digital Signature?.

Note: SAP supports soft certificates only, roaming certificates are not supported. The digital certificate extensions supported are PFX and P12.

Add Keypair

More Information

Suppose that your company has several company codes representing headquarters and subsidiary units. In this case, you can choose to use only one digital certificate, which is registered with the TIN of headquarters or a leading subsidiary. The TIN associated with the common certificate is referred to as intermediary or intermediary representative. See For companies that have subsidiaries, does digital certificate need to be registered for each subsidiary?.

If you use different SAP systems at the same time, such as SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA, you can still use the same digital certificate.

Also remember that a digital certificate has an expiration date. You must renew your certificate when it's expiring soon. See Do I need to renew a digital certificate?. Otherwise, you'll encounter the following errors:

  • Certificate is not valid according to the chain of trust validation or has been issued by an untrusted certificate authority.
  • Signing time is not within validity period.

We hope that you find this information useful. Feel free to leave a comment on this blog or follow SAP Document and Reporting Compliance | SAP Community.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to create logistics projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project System?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Logistics Projects in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
In this Learning Journey you will learn how to create, plan and execute logistics projects in SAP S/4HANA EPPM Project System. You will experience the end-to-end business process, from creation of projects structures, planning of costs, materials and revenues, to execution of projects, realization of revenues, results analysis and period-end closing.

Learning objectives

Upon completing this Learning Journey you will be able to:

  • Create logistics projects using templates.
  • Create and maintain networks and activities within Work Breakdown Structures (WBS).
  • Carry out cost and revenue planning of logistics projects.
  • Carry out material procurement for logistics projects.
  • Release and execute logistics projects.
  • Run period-end closing activities including results analysis and settle projects to profitability analysis.
  • Use reporting features in SAP S/4HANA to analyzes structure, revenues, costs and contribution margin of logistics projects.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: S/4HANA Group Reporting Analytics – Reporting Item API and MDA based Analytical Reports
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we will discuss about the new Reporting Item API of Group Reporting that is planned to be delivered with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1.  We will also show how  to create Hierarchy variables and restricted structure members for Group Reporting queries using Custom analytical queries and Services application. Additionally, we will demo on how to publish the Group Reporting based custom query in Multidimensional Analysis Grid.

 

Reporting Item API: API_CNSLDTNRLBSDGRPJRNLITM

This new API contains the Reporting Item as a field and displays it member values along with key figures and other fields. In the API without reporting item: API_CNSLDTNGRPJRNLITEM there were 3 hierarchy parameters. In this API, we have added two more  parameters: P_ConsolidationRptgItemHier and P_ConsolidationRptgRuleID. Similar to the analytical applications, for e.g. Group Data Analysis with Reporting Rules, you need to provide the ConsolidationReportingItem hierarchy  and ConsolidationReportingRuleID that are already defined in the Define Reporting Rules. Similarly, you need to provide it in the API and you can look at the sample URI:

sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CNSLDTNRLBSDGRPJRNLITM/CnsldtnRuleBsdGrpJrnlItem(P_ConsolidationUnitHierarchy='%24',P_ConsolidationPrftCtrHier='%24',P_ConsolidationSegmentHier='%24',P_KeyDate=datetime'2024-12-31T00:00:00',P_ConsolidationRptgItemHier='X2',P_ConsolidationRptgRuleID='Y10')/Results?$select=ConsolidationUnit,FinancialStatementItem,FiscalYearPeriod,ConsolidationReportingItem,ProfitCenter,SubItem,AmountInGroupCurrency&$filter=ConsolidationVersion eq 'Y10' and PeriodMode eq 'YTD' and ConsolidationGroup eq 'CG2' and FiscalYear eq '2022'

In the Video below, you will see a demo on how you can use this API for SAC using the ODATA Service:

 

Multidimensional Analysis Grid Using Group Reporting Data Source

You can now publish the Group Reporting Custom query in Multidimensional Analysis Grid. The pre-requisites are:

  • Analytical Query of type V3 (CDS Analytical Projection Views)
  • INA Service

The queries that are created in the new app: "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" are of type V3. You can watch a demo below on how to create the V3 query:

You will notice that in the above demo you can create Variables, provide fixed values for dimensions, choose dimensions and key figures as per your use case. Most of it was possible in the previous release also using the app.: Custom Analytical Queries (but of type V1 query).

You can also create a FS Item hierarchy variables as shown below:

The variables can also be used in a Restricted measure or Key figure. It is possible to create restricted structure member in the custom query. Please watch the below demo that illustrates the creation of Current Year VS Previous Year for Amount in Group Currency.

Now its time to create an INA service. Please make sure the custom query is published before creating an INA Service. In the app. "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" click the link "Manage InA Services " and follow the video below to create a service:

In the above example, you will see that the InA service is created out the custom query that we created in the above demo. 

Please now load the app "Manage KPI and Reports" and select the tab "Multidimensional Reports" and click the create button as shown below:

You can select the Custom query and INA Service and publish it as shown below:

While publishing you need to select the Business Catalog and you can create a Custom Tile.

You can see the results on loading the app. based on Multidimensional Analysis grid:

 

Conclusion

Please feel free to share your thoughts by commenting on this blog. Also, we can use this blog as a communication tool for any clarification that you require on this topic. You can also follow my profile for more content on the analytical application for group reporting. I would recommend that you post your questions by specifying the tag: “SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting”.

entry.title: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding application logic with CAP while keeping the core clean
entry.content:
This blog post is the seventh in a series of posts that cover the connectivity options available for SAP Build Apps to interface with SAP systems. The series will cover connecting SAP Build Apps with the following:

  1. CAP-based services
  2. S/4HANA systems
  3. ABAP systems

The format of this post is similar to a tutorial. However, I'll provide more comprehensive details, tips and the opportunity for you to provide feedback. Based on the feedback we will be able to adjust and enhance this post and future parts of the series, and might even be able to improve the products involved as well. 

Introduction 

Before we get into the details of setting up connections between SAP Build Apps and SAP Systems, I intend to introduce a couple of SAP technology components in each post that are relevant for the use cases covered in the series.

Custom business logic

Custom logic in CAP allows you to extend and customise the default behaviours of the framework a fully tailored and functional application. This custom code allows you to implement data validation, enrichment, calculations, integration with external systems, or any other custom processing that your application requires.

In the above figure, the Service API for custom logic, you see four types of APIs. More details can be found in the Learning Journey "Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP". In this blog post, we will focus on the custom event handlers.

Use case G : Adding application logic to the CAP service

For this use case, we'll use SAP Build Code to enhance the CAP service exposing the data source from an S/4 HANA Cloud system and use it in the application previously created in SAP Build Apps. We will also use the Joule assistant to create some code for us. There will surely be CODING involved here, but it will be minimal. This is a typical scenario for so called side-by-side extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Solution diagram

We will start with a quick look into what needs to change in our solution overview. For this use case, the change is relatively small. I've added some app logic that handles the data coming from the S/4HANA Cloud sandbox system. We have already rerouted the data flow in the previous blog post. By routing the data source through our CAP service we can add application logic without changing anything on the S/4HANA system.


The application logic editor

We will now go ahead and add logic to the data service for the entity MySupplier. This service entity exposes a limited set of data entities coming from the S/4HANA Cloud sandbox system. Select MySupplier on the Storyboard and select Add Logic.

The Application Logic Editor opens and a dialog is shown to add a logic module. The logic will be invoked by an event handler. The code for the event handler itself will be generated for you; so you just need to focus on the logic itself.

We'll use the defaults here and select Add.

You will now see the editor, with your new logic module selected. 

Configuring the application logic

There are a few things you can configure. One category is the phase in which the code is triggered during request processing. The other is which standard event it is associated with. For more details, please refer to the CAP documentation.

Phase

Here you can select the phase in which the event handler triggers your code:

Before
As the name suggests, in this case the code will be activated before the service executes it's task. A common use case is the validation of user input. 

On
This is the phase where the event handler actually fulfills a request, such as reading or writing data to or from a database. An example use case here is the creation of mashups or returning mockup data.

After
This code is activated after the service executed it's task and results have been retrieved. A typical use case here is to enrich outbound data.

Standard Event

Your logic can be associated with one or more events related to Create, Read, Update and Delete.


Adding the code

For our example use case, we are adding logic to handle the requests for retrieving data from the S/4HANA Cloud sandbox system. As mentioned before, this data source is read-only. Therefore, our example here will be limited to the READ requests and handle the data after receiving it.

Select Open Code Editor and pick Application Logic.

This opens the code editor, and along side it, for those using SAP Build Code, the SAP Joule AI assistant will be ready for your prompt. In this example I will use Joule. Those using SAP Business Application Studio can just copy-paste the code into the code editor for the same result. 

As you might have noticed while browsing through the data retrieved from the S/4HANA Cloud sandbox system, this contains quite some test data, including plenty of suppliers with the name "Supplier". For the sake of this blog post, we will change the response data before it is sent to the SAP Build Apps client application. This is just simple example of what is possible, without making things too complicated.

I will Accept the code proposed by Joule, which will be copied into my application logic module.

As you might have noticed, Joule will now also offer a button to Open Debug Console. This can help you in debugging your (or Joule's) code. I'll come back to the topic of debugging later. Please be aware that code generated with Generative AI is not always perfect. As mentioned, Joule is an assistant and you as developer must check what your assistant is doing.

While we are at it, let me add another piece of code (manually). In case the SupplierName contains the word "test", we add a fake VAT registration number for that specific record. The end-result is this piece of logic:

 

 

/**
 * Example of service logic to alter query results.
 * Where the SupplierName contains "Supplier", we change it to "Acme Corporation" (Meep Meep...) and add a VAT registration number.
 * Where the SupplierName contains "test", we add a VAT registration number.
 * @After(event = { "READ" }, entity = "blogproductssvc.MySupplier")
 *  {(Object|Object[])} results - For the After phase only: the results of the event processing
 *  {Object} request - User information, tenant-specific CDS model, headers and query parameters
 */
module.exports = async function(results, request) {
    // Check if results is an array, if not make it an array for uniform processing
    const suppliers = Array.isArray(results) ? results : [results];

    // Iterate over each supplier and modify the SupplierName and VATRegistration if condition is met
    suppliers.forEach(supplier => {
        if (supplier.SupplierName && supplier.SupplierName.includes("Supplier")) {
            supplier.SupplierName = "Acme Corporation";
            supplier.VATRegistration = "US12345678";
        }

		if (supplier.SupplierName && supplier.SupplierName.includes("test")) {
            supplier.VATRegistration = "2001234";
        }
    });
}

 

So, where does this code end up in your project? The above code is stored in srv/code, as you can find in the Explorer.

There is also code generated for you (the other highlight files). The srv/service.js handles the initialisation and registers your code with the handler(s). There is also an xml file (using extension .code) that is used by the Application Logic Editor.

Preview the results

Let us see whether this works by starting a Preview. Open the Run Configuration tab and hit Run for the Business Partner (A2X) entry.

The project preview will open. We can now select the 'View as code' button for MySupplier.

In the output, you will find the changed data.

Alternatively, you can run the preview app.


Debugging your code

What if you've created a bug in the logic code? Well, you can easily debug this in the SAP Build Code environment.

In the code editor, you can set a breakpoint in front of the line where you want break execution and inspect what is going on in your JavaScript function.

When you now run your project and trigger your code (by requesting data) the execution will stop in SAP Build Code. In the screenshot below, you can see the breakpoint that was hit. In the debug console you can see the connection details. In the Run and Debug panel you can see local variables, items I've put on the Watch list and also the call stack.

Debugging your code in this SAP Build Code (or SAP Business Application Studio) environment is a lot easier than debugging your service once it is deployed to BTP. So I highly recommend debugging your service's application logic here, using the Preview function.

Deploy to BTP and run the SAP Build Apps preview app

Now that we are done coding, let's deploy it to BTP and go back to SAP Build Apps to see how our data is received in our UI application.

 

Summary – use case G

In this use case we have added custom application logic code to our data service handling data coming from an S/4HANA Cloud 'sandbox' ERP system. As the sandbox system's data services are read-only, we have implemented a simple piece code that changes records after the READ operation is done. 

We have used Joule as our code assistant to generate some of this logic.

It was also demonstrated how easy it is to debug your custom application logic within SAP Build Code, without even deploying your application to BTP. 

What's next

In the next blog post, we will continue on the connectivity topic and change the UI application into a CRUD application.

entry.title: Automating Username Generation for Employee BP Records mandatory in SAP EHS
entry.content:
In the context of SAP EHS (Environment, Health, and Safety), ensuring that all employees have a unique Business Partner (BP) record is crucial for effective management and compliance. Each employee must have a username to be assigned as part of their BP data, which is essential for proper system integration and user access.

This guide demonstrates how to create an ABAP program that automatically generates usernames for employees. The usernames are derived from the first letter of the employee’s first name and their last name, followed by a numeric suffix to ensure uniqueness if similar usernames already exist. This is particularly useful in the SAP EHS module, where each employee's BP record requires a unique identifier.

1. Access the ABAP Editor (SE38)

  • Transaction Code: SE38
  • Open the SAP GUI and enter the transaction code SE38 in the command field.

2. Create a New Program

  • In the ABAP Editor: Initial Screen, enter a name for your program (e.g., ZUPDATE_SYNAME) in the Program field.
  • Click the Create button.

3. Define Program Attributes

  • Title: Enter a meaningful title for your report (e.g., "Update SYNAME for Active Employees").
  • Type: Choose Executable Program since this is a standalone report that will be executed directly.
  • Status: Typically, this would be set to Test Program during development and Production Program when finalized.
  • Application: Select the appropriate application area, such as HR if the program is related to human resources.

4. Write the ABAP Code

  • In the ABAP Editor, you can now write or paste the code provided for your report.

The ABAP Code : 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZUPDATE_SYNAME
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZUPDATE_SYNAME.

DATA: lt_employees TYPE TABLE OF pa0002,
      ls_employee  TYPE pa0002.

DATA: lt_pa0001 TYPE TABLE OF pa0001,
      ls_pa0001 TYPE pa0001.

DATA: lt_info_0105 TYPE TABLE OF pa0105,
      ls_info_0105 TYPE pa0105.

DATA: lv_counter TYPE string,
      lv_suffix  TYPE string,
      lv_base_uname TYPE string,
      lv_final_uname TYPE string,
      existing_uname TYPE pa0105-uname.

* Check the current date
WRITE: / 'Current date:', sy-datum.

* Select PERNR from PA0001 where ENDDA is greater than or equal to today
SELECT * FROM pa0001 INTO TABLE lt_pa0001
  WHERE endda >= sy-datum.

* Check how many records were selected
DATA(lv_count) = lines( lt_pa0001 ).
WRITE: / 'Number of records found:', lv_count.

* Now, use the results from lt_pa0001 to select from PA0002
IF lt_pa0001 IS NOT INITIAL.
  SELECT * FROM pa0002 INTO TABLE lt_employees
    FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_pa0001
    WHERE pernr = lt_pa0001-pernr.
ENDIF.

* Check if LT_EMPLOYEES has records
IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lt_employees IS NOT INITIAL.
  LOOP AT lt_employees INTO ls_employee.

    CLEAR: lt_info_0105, lv_counter, lv_base_uname, lv_final_uname.

    SELECT * FROM pa0105 INTO TABLE lt_info_0105
      WHERE pernr = ls_employee-pernr
      AND endda >= sy-datum.

    IF lt_info_0105 IS INITIAL.

      lv_base_uname = ls_employee-vorna(1) && ls_employee-nachn.

      lv_counter = 1.

      WHILE lv_counter < 1000.

        CONCATENATE lv_base_uname lv_counter INTO lv_final_uname.

        SELECT SINGLE uname FROM pa0105
          INTO @existing_uname
          WHERE uname = _final_uname.

        IF sy-subrc <> 0.
          EXIT.
        ENDIF.

        lv_counter = lv_counter + 1.

      ENDWHILE.

      CLEAR ls_info_0105.
      ls_info_0105-pernr = ls_employee-pernr.
      ls_info_0105-endda = '99991231'. " Set to the max date
      ls_info_0105-begda = sy-datum.
      ls_info_0105-subty = '0001'.
      ls_info_0105-aedtm = sy-datum.
      ls_info_0105-uname = SY-uname.
      ls_info_0105-usrty = '0001'.
      ls_info_0105-usrid = lv_final_uname.

      INSERT pa0105 FROM ls_info_0105.

      WRITE: / 'Created new UNAME for Employee ID:', ls_employee-pernr,
             'UNAME:', ls_info_0105-uname.

    ELSE.

      LOOP AT lt_info_0105 INTO ls_info_0105.

        IF ls_info_0105-uname IS INITIAL.

          lv_base_uname = ls_employee-vorna(1) && ls_employee-nachn.

          lv_counter = 1.

          WHILE lv_counter < 1000.

            CONCATENATE lv_base_uname lv_counter INTO lv_final_uname.

            SELECT SINGLE uname FROM pa0105
              INTO @existing_uname
              WHERE uname = _final_uname.

            IF sy-subrc <> 0.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.

            lv_counter = lv_counter + 1.

          ENDWHILE.

          ls_info_0105-uname = lv_final_uname.
          MODIFY pa0105 FROM ls_info_0105.

          WRITE: / 'Updated UNAME for Employee ID:', ls_employee-pernr,
                 'New UNAME:', ls_info_0105-uname.

        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'No employee records found for the given PERNRs.'.
ENDIF.

* Display message or log completion
WRITE: / 'UNAME update completed.'.

 

5. Save the Program

  • Click Save (Ctrl+S) to save your program.
  • You will be prompted to choose a package. If this is a test or temporary program, you can save it in the $TMP package, which is for local objects that don't need to be transported. Otherwise, choose an appropriate package.
  • Transport Request: If you’re saving it in a package that requires transport, you’ll need to assign it to an existing transport request or create a new one.

6. Check and Activate the Program

  • Check: Click the Check button (Ctrl+F2) to ensure there are no syntax errors in your program.
  • Activate: Once the program is error-free, click the Activate button (Ctrl+F3). Activation makes the program executable in the system.

7. Execute the Program

  • After activation, you can execute the program directly from SE38 by clicking the Execute button (F8).
  • You may also run it by entering the program name in the command field from the SAP Easy Access screen.

entry.title: Split Valuation in SAP MM
entry.content:
Introduction:

Split valuation is a feature in SAP Material Management (MM) that enables companies to manage and assign different values to various segments of the same material within the same valuation area (such as a plant or company code). This functionality is only applicable when using the moving average price control, meaning that materials subject to split valuation can only be valued using the moving average price method.

Reasons for Implementing Split Valuation:

  • Different Material Origins: When a material comes from different sources.
  • Variations in Quality: When the same material exists in different grades of quality.
  • Material Status Differences: When the material has different statuses.
  • In-house vs. External Procurement: Differentiating between materials produced internally and those procured from external suppliers.
  • Variation in Deliveries: Differentiating between different deliveries of the same material.

Valuation and Accounting Considerations:

Each valuation type within split valuation can have its own distinct valuation price. This allows for financial differentiation even if the material code remains the same across items. The valuation can vary based on factors such as the supplier or quality of the material, directly impacting accounting and costing processes. For instance, different batches can be assigned different prices, reflecting their unique characteristics.

Use Cases:

Split valuation is particularly beneficial in industries where the cost of materials is heavily influenced by procurement or production conditions. This is common in sectors such as pharmaceuticals, chemicals, or food and beverages, where different batches may originate from various sources or be produced under varying conditions, resulting in differing values.

Benefits of Split Valuation:

  • Enhanced Costing Accuracy: By assigning different values to stocks based on specific characteristics, companies can achieve more accurate costing and pricing strategies.
  • Improved Inventory Management: Businesses can better manage inventories with diverse origins, qualities, or other attributes, ensuring efficient and effective stock utilization.
  • Regulatory Compliance: In highly regulated industries, like pharmaceuticals, correctly tracking and valuing batches is crucial for compliance.
  • Risk Management: By categorizing stocks according to valuation types, companies can better manage risks related to price fluctuations, supplier reliability, or quality differences.

Split Valuation needs to be activated before setting any other configuration. Follow the steps given below to activate split valuation

Path to Activate Split Valuation

IMG ⇒ Materials Management ⇒ Valuation and Account Assignment ⇒ Split Valuation ⇒ Activate Split Valuation

TCode: OMW0

Step 1 − On the Display IMG screen, select Activate Split Valuation by following the above path.

Step 2 − Click the option Split Material Valuation Active. Click on Save. Split valuation is now activated.

Some important terms under split valuation are as follows.

  • Valuation Area − Material may be valuated at plant level or at company code level. The level at which materials are valuated is known as valuation area.
  • Valuation Category − Valuation category is the criteria on which material is split valuated.
  • Valuation Type − Valuation type specifies the features of a valuation category.

Split Valuation Configuration

Follow the steps given below to do split valuation configuration such as defining valuation category and valuation type.

Path to Configure Split Valuation

IMG ⇒ Materials Management ⇒ Valuation and Account Assignment ⇒ Split Valuation ⇒ Configure Split Valuation

T Code: OMWC

Step 1 − On the Display IMG screen, select Configure Split Valuation by following the above path.

Step 2 − Select Global Types to create valuation type.

Step 3 − Select the Create 

Step 4 − Provide the name of the valuation type. Click on Save. A new Valuation Type will be created.

Step 5 − Now go to the same screen and click Global Categories to create valuation category.

Step 6 − Select the Create 

Step 7 − Provide the name of the valuation category and Attributes. Select Save. A new Valuation Category will be created.

Step 8 – Select Valuation Category and Click the Types -->Cat.

Step 9 – Select respective Valuation Type for above selected Valuation Category and Select Activate.

Step 10 − Now go to the same screen and Select Local Definitions to map valuation type and valuation category.

Step 11 − Select the Cats. → OU 

Step 12 – select valuation category set the status as active, and then click the Activate. Valuation category and valuation area are now mapped and both are activated.

Material Creation with Split Valuation

1.    Create Material  (MM01)

Create material with required views.

Mention details in required field in all selected views

And in Accounting View 1, should specify the valuation category

Step 2: Create material with Valuation type

Specify Material Number and select only Accounting View 1

Mention the Valuation Type,

 

 

 

Material has created with valuation Type

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA integration with FSM setup activities
entry.content:
Introduction:

Integrating SAP Field Service Management (FSM) with SAP s/4HANA is crucial for optimizing field service operation and achieving seamless and data flow across systems. In this blog, we will go through the steps involved in setting up the SAP S4 HANA and FSM cloud for newly created company.

System requirements:

  • SAP S4 HANA On-Premise.
  • SAP FSM Cloud account.
  • SAP Cloud connector(YAML file / Message broker)

 

Steps involved:

  • E4C Connector setup in S4.
  • Configure SAP S4.
  • Create FSM company in cloud using E4C cloud manager.
  • Message broker configuration .
  • Configure FSM cloud.

 

E4C Connector setup in S4:

Integrating SAP S/4HANA with FSM using the E4C connector is essential for enabling seamless service management and operational efficiency. This section outlines the steps to configure and activate the E4C connector for this specific integration.

  1. Logical system:

A logical system is an application system in which the applications are coordinated to work in one common database. In SAP terms, a logical system corresponds to a client. The logical system is important in the following SAP areas- Communication between two or more logical systems.
We can create the logical system using below T-code : SALE > Basic Settings > Logical Systems > Define Logical systems.

Logical System 

Short Description 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

 

  1. Distribution model:

The Distribution Model defines the message type of data to be exchanged. The distribution model consists of separate model views in which all the associated, cross-system message flows in your organization can be defined. These views must be distributed to the receiving systems.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : SALE > Distribution Model > Edit > Create Model View.

Model 

Short Description 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

 

  1. Partner:

Partner profiles are a prerequisite for data exchange. This involves defining who can exchange messages with the SAP system and using which port. The partner profile contains parameters that define the electronic interchange of data with a partner via that IDoc interface.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : WE20.

Partner No 

Partner Type 

TY 

Agent (Technical User) 

Lang 

(System name)

  

(User_name) 

EN 

 

  1. Port:

The port defines the technical characteristics of the connection between SAP and the Subsystem. It also defines the medium in which data is exchanged between the two systems.

We can create the logical system using below T-code : WE21.

Port 

Short Description 

Function Module 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

/PACG/ECM_S_PORT  

 

  1. Configure web service:

A web service is any piece of software that makes itself available over the internet and uses a standardized XML messaging system. XML is used to encode all communications to a web service.

T-code: SM80 Go to Package - /PACG/ECM  

  • Service Consumer:  CLAS/PACG/CO_ECMISEND_OUTBOUND_MES

 

  • Go to SOA MANAGER 

Tcode: SOAMANAGER -> Web Service Configuration > Object Name: /PACG/CO_ECMISEND_OUTBOUND_MES 

  • Here we will be using the same logical Port we created earlier.

Logical Port Name 

Description 

Logical Port is default 

(System name)

FSM Cloud Connection 

/PACG/ECM_S_PORT  

  • Consumer Security Point :Maintain the Username and Password, this will be used in Message Broker.
  • Message Broker URL Components : Protocol, Path, Port, Host.
  • SOAP Protocol : Please choose the below while setting up the service in SOA Manager 

Message ID Protocol 

Data Transfer Scope 

Suppress ID Transfer 

Minimal Data Transfer 



 

Configuration in SAP S4:

Here we can see the detailed steps and T-codes required for setting up the objects in  SAP S/4HANA for newly created company.
we can use the FSM connector i,e /N/PACG/EC-> Customizing -> Objects. Or we can use the direct T-codes.

T-code

Object name

Object description

/n/pacg/ecm_eodef

General Properties of E4C 

The content of this table is generated by a setup program which defines all the objects available in FSM Cloud 

/n/pacg/ecm_cprop

ERP Object Definition 

Customize general, company-independent properties of FSM Cloud Connector.

/n/pacg/ecm_cmsgtype

External Message Type Definition 

This is used to get the assignment between external message  type and SAP Message type. This is a kind of the translation from the outside world to  the internal SAP common language 

/n/pacg/ecm_eoas

Object Assignment 

The content of this table is generated by the setup program provided by Proaxia. Definition of all E4C object types available in SAP.

/n/pacg/ecm_saasg

Sales Area Assignment 

Here you can define the cloud company against Sales Organization, Distribution channel and Division.

/n/pacg/ecm_plas

Plant Assignment 

We can define the plant that will be used in cloud for material consumption. Objects are sent from SAP to cloud companies. Cloud Companies are reflected in E4C Companies.

/n/pacg/ecm_paas

Personnel Area Assignment 

The personnel area is an organizational unit that represents a specific area of the enterprise and is organized according to aspects of personnel, time management and payroll

/n/pacg/ecm_clao

Allowed Checklist Objects 

The mapping between the E4C Object Type which can be used by the Checklists and the corresponding SAP Object Type. It is used to create a GOS-Link between the Checklist and the referenced object.

/n/pacg/ecm_cmsgon

Configuration of Online Calls 

Cloud message type – this external message type name is used to get the assignment between external message type and SAP message type

/n/pacg/ecm_comp

Company Definition 

Company can be defined as a business unit which can be used in the system as a primary note for all business operation

/n/pacg/ecm_caac

Company account assignment 

This links the cloud account to the company Id and logical port . Each company ID is connected to a i system and logical port at the platform level by the Basis team. The combination of logical system and logical port controls the data traffic from S4 system to the company ID. The data traffic also controls the movement of data from the FSM cloud to the technicians’ mobile device.

/n/pacg/ecm_acurc

Company code for allowed currencies 

Company for which currencies is restricted. This will be set as one company ID per currency that is allowed for that company

/n/pacg/ecm_acur

Allowed Currencies 

For a company ID the allowed currencies are maintained here.

/n/pacg/ecm_acou

Allowed Countries 

For a company ID the allowed countries are maintained here.

/n/pacg/ecm_ccust 

General Customizing for Customer 

The company ID is linked to customer standard and non-standard bill-to and Ship-to address

/n/pacg/ecm_ktokdmap

Customer Account Group Mapping 

Customer account group in each company is mapped to an external account group. A customer account group can be assigned to a number range with external or internal numbering as a default.

/n/pacg/ecm_eq1

Equipment Setting 

Here we enable the equipment and Functional locations (Telo is defined here as functional location) are transferred to the cloud. In FSM, Equipment data and function location data are transferred to the same master data table which is mapped to the equipment object. Whenever there are mater data needs to be imported from S4 to FSM, the equi&Telo settings are changed to “Equipment and Functional Location”.

/n/pacg/ecm_cinc01

Include Types of Equipment’s 

This includes types of equipment categories that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_cinc02

Include Types of Technical Location 

This includes types of Functional locations that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_eqsm

 Equipment Cloud Status Mapping 

 The includes the equipment and functional locations status that are sent to cloud

/n/pacg/ecm_itype

 Item – Material Group and Company Assignment 

On material type level we can define the material type which needs to be transmitted to the cloud for each company ID

PACG/ECM_ICONF

Item - Item Configuration

 On item configuration we can define if the material should be reflected  to FSM as batch or Normal based on the check MBEW setup 

/n/pacg/ecm_ibs

Item – Batch or Serial numbers 

Item type to FSM to distinguish batch and serial materials 

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhquant

Item – Quantities to send to cloud 

Here we define which stock types needs to be sent to the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_iwlon

Item Warehouse Level – Good Movement Send Online 

You must define which Movement Types are relevant to be sent to the cloud. Movement Type 261 (GI for Order) is used to transfer Goods from Warehouse when the Technician consumes the Goods from FSM. A Material Document is created in S4 to record the Goods Movement.

/n/pacg/ecm_rmgm

Item Warehouse Level – Reserved Material: Action on GM 

If a Component is entered in a Service order a material reservation is created in E4C and sent to the cloud. This is a pre-warning that a shipment is outstanding.

/n/pacg/ecm_active

E4C Activity – General Activity Property 

The general details of an activity that belongs to a service call setup is done here.

/n/pacg/ecm_acti_type

E4C Activity – Activity Type 

Cloud Data Model offers certain Activity Types – for scheduling purposes the type “ASSIGNMENT” is in use. In SAP we define a counterpart.

/n/pacg/ecm_acti_statu

E4C Activity E4C Activity Status 

The ERP status and the Cloud status are mapped here.

/n/pacg/ecm_actmae

E4C Activity – SAP Activity Code Mapping 

Map internal & external activity type. Here reminder defines a general reminder notifying the user in mobile before activity start.

/n/pacg/ecm_extscpdef

Service call – External Priority Definition 

Definition of the values and description of the order priority in the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_screq

Service call – Service Call Sending – Requirements 

Definition of the status that must be achieved before sending the order to the cloud. We distinguish between Initial load (= Full sync) or the standard delta (changes only) transmission mode

/n/pacg/ecm_scstd

Service call – Status of Service Call 

Mapping of an SAP-System status of a Service order to the possible Cloud statuses. Mapping System status of a Service order in combination with priority, Status and description for the cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_scg

Service call – Service Call General Settings 

If this is set here, then activities/operations are listed in the effort type for technician while working on the activity. To avoid them please do not check this option.

/n/pacg/ecm_sctd

Service Call – Service Call Type Definition 

Here we can set up the different service call type and its parameters that will be sent to cloud based on company

/n/pacg/ecm_sctdm

Service call – Movements for Service Call Type

The below table defines the Goods Movement types that are allowed to cloud.

/n/pacg/ecm_scpriomap

Service Call – SAP Service Call Priority Mapping

Key that determines which priorities are allowed for a notification type.

/n/pacg/ecm_tkdef

Service Call – Task Control Key Activation

In this step, we maintain the allowed control keys for the individual operations of the maintenance and service orders.

/n/pacg/ecm_actgs

Service Call – Activity Feedback – [General settings] 

In this step, we configure feedback codes for three level for each company codes.

/n/pacg/ecm_sercd

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Code Groups]

In this step, we maintain the various code groups and levels of their appearance in FSM. These are required to update the service notification in S4.

/n/pacg/ecm_serfp

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Level Nr] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of levels to code groups. These are required to update the service notification in S4 to the correct code groups. 

/n/pacg/ecm_secma

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Activity Codes] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of codes to material(profile). This setting is required to filter codes based on profile related to Functional location in FSM.

/n/pacg/ecm_segma

Service Call – Activity Feedback [Activity Group] 

In this step, we maintain the mapping of code groups to material(profile). This setting is required to filter code groups based on profile related to Functional location in FSM.

/n/pacg/ecm_sasa

Service Call – Assignment Status  

In this step, we maintain the user status with the operation workflow step of FSM. The workflow step is updated in operation user status of the service order.

/n/pacg/ecm_snot_gener

General properties of service notification

Define determination of plant for deciding a notification is sending relevant.

/n/pacg/ecm_snot_types

 Notification types 

Defines Mapping of Service Notification per company and type to identify Customer, Contact Person, Responsible Person and the source for long text. 

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhgen

Warehouse – General settings 

Definition of warehouses which are relevant for sending warehouse description to FSM

/n/pacg/ecm_wrhouse

 Warehouse - E4C Warehouse 

Definition of warehouses which are relevant for sending, master data, stock level and material movements

/n/pacg/ecm_ctet

 Work Time and Time Effort – Time Effort Type 

With this customizing you can define the different Work time effort types which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_canab

Work Time and Time Effort – CATS attendance or absence type

With this customizing you can define the different activity types associated with attendance/absence type which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_vwrktc

 Work Time and Time Effort – Work time Task Definition 

With this customizing you can define the different Work times and map to attendance/absence type which can be selected within the App, and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction

/n/pacg/ecm_chop

 Work Time and Time Effort – Charge Options 

With this customizing you can define if the effort is of charge types which can be selected within the App and with which attributes they are posted either in CATS or as confirmation against service order defined in this transaction. The Description field is blank to allow notes from technician to be posted to CATS.

/n/pacg/ecm_udf_conf

reports UDF – UDF Definitions 

Workforce Management handles a User Defined Field (UDF) where the SAP FSM Cloud Connector will send commands. UDF (User Defined Fields) In SAP FSM, we can use UDF functionality to define Custom Fields. UDF fields can be created to fit scenarios and align closely with business needs. The UDF fields can store data in the cloud for automation, validation, dashboards and reporting purposes. 

/n/pacg/ecm_userg

Users – General Settings

This setting is established in the connector to identify which field of the User data is mapped with the Company Code. By setting this configuration admin will use the User data (ID) to send the data from S4 to FSM

/n/pacg/ecm_user

Users – Maintenance View

List of all users sent to FSM application will be maintained in this section. Only users’ data which are available in this table will be sent to FSM using Send programs. 

 

 

Create FSM company in cloud using E4C cloud manager:

Login to the cloud account in where in which system you would like to create the FSM company by providing the cloud account and its password.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Message broker configuration:

As per SAP architecture, Message broker is the middleware to connect SAP S4 and SAP FSM cloud system. Message broker is a hardware installed which is used  for installing the software component for the connector setup. 

This will be done by Basis team where they add newly created company name mapped with logical system and logical port and restart the MB service.

 

Configure FSM cloud:

  • Login in SAP FSM admin by filling in Account name, username, and password.

 

  • Navigate to Companies after logging in.

 

  • After the company is created, it will appear in companies’ section as shown in the below screenshot. 

     

 

  • Translations: The Translations tab is used to create and manage custom translations that can be used in different places in the application, such as in screen configurations, in business rule actions, or even in widgets for a given company. The Translations tab can be accessed by navigating to Admin > Company > Translations. Here we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

 

 

 

  • Company settings: Here we can customize company settings as per the requirements. Also, we can create new / Download and upload existing from any other existing companies.

 



  • Business rules: Go to the reference company and select Business Rules, Select the business rules that are required for the new company based on requirement and download it Upload the downloaded business rules in the newly created company.

 

  • Screen configuration: Here we can create custom rules as per the requirements . These can be Downloaded and uploaded to any other existing companies as required. Also, initially we can find set of already defines business rules by SAP.

 



  • Service workflow: The steps which we define here will be available in FSM Mobile Application. The Service Workflow feature can be reached from Admin > Company > Service Workflow. All users with access to the admin app can access this feature.

 

Once you clicked on create, you will find the options to add a name to your service workflow, to make that workflow as default and you can provide name, description & indicate next step for the steps which you add. By clicking the eye icon beside the step, you can edit the data for that step.

 

  • Custom objects: Go to the reference company and select Custom objects->Custom field definitions, you can create a custom object manually here by clicking on the create.

Note: All the other Custom field definitions would be configured and sent from S4(UDF) to FSM, we can create manually only if its required.

 

  • Checklist: Go to Shell Application and click on Smartforms and feedback for the reference company.

 

Click on Templates and all the created checklists would appear.

 

From Reference company, Double clicks on the checklist and from the 3 dots, click on export XML, which will download the checklist.

and click on the + sign in the bottom right corner, where you will be getting an option to add the category name upon clicking on ok, new category will be created. And on your right side under the import templates section, you can upload the downloaded file for your newly created category. By double clicking on the file name inside category you will be redirected to new page where you can activate the checklist by releasing it.

Note- For the new company, this checklist would not be appearing and would be empty as this is a reference image from the reference company.

  • Maintain the Checklist: A After releasing the checklist in Shell, go to tcode - se16- /PACG/ECM_CLTTH and give company ID and execute it.

 

 

In another tab, go to tcode - sm30 – Custom checklist table tcode and maintain it.

 

Go to the new company and edit all the checklist values from another tab i.e.  - se16- Custom checklist table tcode.


Common idoc message types which involved in FSM:

Outbound idoc: It’s an intermediate document that is sent from SAP system to FSM cloud.

Outbound Message Type

Inbound idoc: It’s an intermediate document that is sent from FSM cloud/Application  to SAP system.

/PACG/ECM_SERVNOTIFIN

inbound  Service Notification

inbound  Service Rating

/PACG/ECM_TOOLASSIN

/PACG/ECM_USEDTOOLIN

/PACG/ECM_WORKTIMEIN
